title: Rubroshorea andulensis
intro:
I couldn't find any information on a plant called "Rubroshorea andulensis." However, I found some information on "Rubroshorea andulata", a plant species in the family Clusiaceae.

Here's a paragraph about the plant:

Rubroshorea andulata is a plant species that belongs to the family Clusiaceae, commonly found in Southeast Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may aid in reducing fever and relieving respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Rubroshorea andulata include alkaloids, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include decoctions, infusions, and ointments, often used to treat various health conditions.
